title: aquilegia vulgaris var. viscosa
intro: Aquilegia vulgaris var. viscosa, also known as sticky columbine, is a plant species in the Ranunculaceae family, characterized by its vibrant blue-purple flowers and sticky stems. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of sticky columbine include its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate pain. The plant can be prepared for medicinal use through various herbal preparations, including infusions, tinctures, and salves.
